evals.prompt:
/ai-kickoff email-classifier
I want to build an AI that classifies incoming customer emails into categories like billing, support, feature-request, and spam. I have a CSV of 500 labeled emails.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded project with adapted field names (email/category instead of input_field/output_field), Literal types for the categories, a CSV-loading data.py, and a weighted metric function.

assertions.name: adapts_field_names
assertions.description: Uses task-specific field names like email and category, not generic input_field/output_field

assertions.name: uses_literal_for_categories
assertions.description: Uses Literal type for the category output field

assertions.name: with_inputs_on_examples
assertions.description: Calls .with_inputs() on all dspy.Example objects in data.py

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: LM config uses generic provider with alternative comment

assertions.name: includes_metric
assertions.description: Generates a metrics.py with a task-specific metric function

assertions.name: csv_data_loading
assertions.description: data.py includes CSV loading logic since the user mentioned a CSV file

evals.prompt:
/ai-kickoff qa-bot
I want to create a question-answering system. Users ask questions about our product docs and the AI answers. I do not have training data yet. I want it served as a REST API.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded project with Q&A fields, ChainOfThought module, server.py with FastAPI endpoints, and guidance to use /ai-generating-data for synthetic training data.

assertions.name: includes_server
assertions.description: Generates server.py with FastAPI routes since user wants a REST API

assertions.name: uses_chain_of_thought
assertions.description: Uses ChainOfThought for Q&A which benefits from reasoning

assertions.name: mentions_data_generation
assertions.description: References /ai-generating-data since user has no training data

assertions.name: adapts_fields_for_qa
assertions.description: Uses question/answer or similar Q&A field names, not generic placeholders

evals.prompt:
/ai-kickoff sentiment-checker
I need a simple yes/no check - is this customer review positive or negative? No API needed, just a script.
evals.expected_output: A minimal scaffolded project using dspy.Predict (not ChainOfThought) since it is a simple binary task, with bool or Literal output type.

assertions.name: uses_predict_not_cot
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Predict instead of ChainOfThought for this simple binary classification

assertions.name: uses_constrained_output
assertions.description: Uses bool or Literal for the binary positive/negative output

assertions.name: no_server_file
assertions.description: Does not generate server.py since user said no API needed

assertions.name: simple_structure
assertions.description: Generates the basic project structure without API-serving extras
